OBJECTION TO MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT FILED BY PLAINTIFF SOLOMUA, PATRICIA

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT WILLIAM SCHECTER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGEMENT ON THE SINGLE CAUSE OF FOR MEDICAL MALPRACTICE IN THE COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF PATRICIA SOLUMUA IS GRANTED. THE UNDISPUTED FACTS SHOW THAT MS. SOLUMUA'S CLAIM WAS TIME-BARRED NO LATER THAN 2014, THREE YEARS BEFORE THIS LAWSUIT WAS FILED. THE UNDISPUTED EVIDENCE IS THAT MS. SOLUMUA CONSULTED WITH ATTORNEYS ABOUT SUING DR. SCHECTER REGARDING THE 1998 SURGERY UP TO AND INCLUDING 2013. THEREFORE, GIVING MS. SOLUMUA EVERY BENEFIT OF THE DOUBT AND DISREGARDING THE UNDISPUTED FACTS SUGGESTING THAT MS. SOLUMUA'S CLAIM WAS LIKELY TIME-BARRED LONG BEFORE THEN, MS. SOLUMUA WAS REQUIRED TO SUE DR. SCHECTER NO LATER THAN THE END OF 2014. ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) PREVAILING/ PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED JUDGMENT IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR#6185, TONYVAUGHN@YAHOO.COM, NOT REPORTED. =(302/HK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant William Schecter, M.D.S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Oct-31-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Warren Webster, Esq. 415-288-9800 of Hassard Bonnington LLP for Defendant.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT WILLIAM SCHECTER, M.D.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.

Defendant William Schecter's motion for summary judgement on the single cause of for medical malpractice in the complaint filed by plaintiff Patricia Solumua is granted. The undisputed facts show that Ms. Solumua's claim was time-barred no later than 2014, three years before this lawsuit was filed. The undisputed evidence is that Ms. Solumua consulted with attorneys about suing Dr. Schecter regarding the 1998 surgery up to and including 2013. Therefore, giving Ms. Solumua every benefit of the doubt and disregarding the undisputed facts suggesting that Ms. Solumua's claim was likely time-barred long before then, Ms. Solumua was required to sue Dr. Schecter no later than the end of 2014.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Prevailing/ party to submit a proposed Judgment in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Not Reported. =(302/HK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Sep-10-2018 Continued To Nov-14-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance by any party or counsel.

DEFENDANT WILLIAM SCHECTER, M.D.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.

There being no appearances, the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant William Schecter, M.D.'s motion for summary judgment is CONTINUED TO NOVEMBER 14, 2018 to allow plaintiff to file a substantive opposition to the motion. Plaintiff may file and serve a substantive opposition that complies with Cal. Code of Civil Procedure 437c by October 31, 2018. A failure to file such an opposition will be deemed a non-opposition and the motion will be granted. Defendant may file and serve a reply by November 8, 2018.

Judge: Anne-Christine Massullo, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600, 415.551.5868, Email: torreanom@aol.com. =(302/ACM)
